title: Proficient trigonometrical-fitted two-derivative multistep collocation methods
author: Lee, Aris, et al.

steps.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps.instruction: In this study, we plan to construct efficient fifth-order two-derivative multistep collocation (TDMC) method with predictor-corrector mode to solve special class of second-order ordinary differential equation in the form of(1)yâ€³(x)=f(x,y(x)),y(x0)=y0,yâ€²(x0)=yâ€²0.

steps.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps.instruction: We begin with the general formulation of TDMC method for solving problem (1), comprises the derivative of the solution yâ€³â€²(x)=g(x,y(x),yâ€²(x))=gx(x,y(x),yâ€²(x))+gy(x,y(x),yâ€²(x))yâ€²+gyâ€²(x,y(x),yâ€²(x))f as follows:(2)âˆ‘j=0kÎ±jy(x+jh)+âˆ‘j=0m1Î±vjy(x+vjh)=h2(âˆ‘j=0kÎ²jfn+j+âˆ‘j=0m2Î²vjfn+vj)+h3(âˆ‘j=0kÎ³jgn+j+âˆ‘j=0m3Î³vjgn+vj),where Î±j,Î±vj,Î²j,Î²vj,Î³j,Î³vjâˆˆR,m1,m2,m3,kâˆˆZ+ and vj is non-integer.

steps.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps.instruction: To construct TDMC method, we approximate the solution by employing the interpolating function with Legendre polynomials serving as basis functions,(3)T0=1,T1=z,T2=32z2âˆ’12,T3=52z3âˆ’32z,T4=358z4âˆ’154z2+38,T5=638z5âˆ’354z3+158z.

steps.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps.instruction: Then, we approximate the solution using an interpolating function (power series) in the following equation:(4)Y(x)=âˆ‘j=0s1+s2+Î·âˆ’1ajTj(x),where xâˆˆ[x0,b],aj is unknown parameter, Tj is Legendre polynomial, s1 and s2 are the number of interpolations for second and third derivative respectively, Î· is distinct collocation point with Î·>0 and b is endpoints of interval.

steps.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps.instruction: Similarly, the second derivative of third derivative of interpolating function will be as follows:Yâ€³(x)=âˆ‘j=0s1+s2+Î·âˆ’1ajTâ€³j(x),(5)Yâ€³â€²(x)=âˆ‘j=0s1+s2+Î·âˆ’1ajTâ€³â€²j(x).

steps.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
steps.instruction: The continuous approximation is formulated by enforcing the following conditions:(6)Y(xn+Î·)=yn+Î·,Î·=0,1,53,Yâ€³(xn+s1)=fn+s1,s1=0,1,53,2,Yâ€³â€²(xn+s2)=gn+s2,s2=0,1,53,2.

steps.instruction: Later, we express Î±j,Î²j,Î³j as continuous coefficients in terms of t by letting t=xâˆ’xnh, the following parameters as obtained as(8)Î±0=1âˆ’t,Î±1=t,Î²0=âˆ’720t+12t2âˆ’14t4+110t5,Î²1=âˆ’320t+14t4âˆ’110t5,Î³0=âˆ’120t+16t3âˆ’16t4+120t5,Î³1=130tâˆ’112t4+120t5.

steps.instruction: Similarly, we simultaneously solve for Y(xn+1),Y(xn+5/3),Yâ€³(xn+1),Yâ€³(xn+5/3),Yâ€³â€²(xn+1),Yâ€³â€²(xn+5/3) to derive new coefficients aj,j=0,1,...,5. Subsequently, by substituting these aj into Eq. (4), we derived continuous method successfully as follows:(11)Y(x)=Î±1yn+1+Î±5/3yn+5/3+h2(Î²1fn+1+Î²5/3fn+5/3)+h3(Î³1gn+1+Î³5/3gn+5/3).

steps.instruction: Subsequently, when we define Î±k,Î²k,Î³k,k=1,53, as continuous functions of t by setting t=xâˆ’xnh, we acquire(12)Î±1=52âˆ’32t,Î±5/3=âˆ’32+32t,Î²1=âˆ’16+649240tâˆ’254t2+458t3âˆ’94t4+2780t5,Î²5/3=1âˆ’32380t+274t2âˆ’458t3+94t4âˆ’2780t5,Î³1=âˆ’73144+1469720tâˆ’258t2+5524âˆ’1316t4+980t5,Î³5/3=âˆ’109432+23272160tâˆ’158t2+138âˆ’1116t4+980t5.

steps.instruction: When t=2, we obtain yn+2 and its derivative (differentiate Eq. (11) with respect to x and t=xâˆ’xnh) as follows:(13)yn+2=âˆ’12yn+1+32yn+5/3+h2(124fn+1+18fn+5/3)+h3(1144gn+1+1432gn+5/3),yâ€²n+2=1h[âˆ’32yn+1+32yn+5/3+h2(49240fn+1+3780fn+5/3)+h3(29720gn+1+1672160gn+5/3)].

steps.title: Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
Derivation of two-derivative multistep collocation method
Trigonometrically-Fitted two-derivative linear multistep method
steps.instruction: In developing the TDMC method with a trigonometric fitting technique, denoted as TF-TDMC method, we replace some coefficients in TDMC method with Ai and Bi,i=1,2,...,6 as follows:(17)yn+53=âˆ’23yn+53yn+1+h2(A1fn+191486fn+1)+h3(A2gn+118gn+1),yn+2=âˆ’12yn+1+32yn+53+h2(124fn+1+A3fn+53)+h3(1144gn+1+A4gn+53),hyâ€²n+53=âˆ’yn+yn+1+h2(B1fn+10071620fn+1)+h3(B2gn+6791620gn+1),hyâ€²n+2=âˆ’32yn+1+32yn+53+h2(49240fn+1+B3fn+53)+h3(29720gn+1+B4gn+53),(18)yn+2=âˆ’12yn+1+32yn+5/3+h2(A5fn+5/3+512fn+2)+h3(A6gn+5/3âˆ’118gn+2),hyâ€²n+2=âˆ’32yn+1+32yn+5/3+h2(B5fn+5/3+4130fn+2)+h3(B6gn+5/3âˆ’31180gn+2).

steps.title: Trigonometrically-Fitted two-derivative linear multistep method
steps.instruction: We set yn+k=eiÎ¸xekiw,k=0,1,53,2 where i is imaginary unit,w=Î¸h and Î¸âˆˆR. Subsequently, we determine the first derivative, yâ€²n+k, second derivative, fn+k and third derivative, gn+k using the formula of yn+k as mentioned above. Then, exponential functions eiÎ¸x and eâˆ’iÎ¸x are integrated at each stage, we obtain the equations corresponding to y,hyâ€² below:(19)eÂ±53iw=âˆ’23+53eÂ±iwâˆ’w2(A1+191486eÂ±iw)âˆ“iw3(A2+118eÂ±iw),eÂ±2iw=âˆ’12eÂ±iw+32eÂ±53iwâˆ’w2(124eÂ±iw+A3eÂ±53iw)âˆ“iw3(1144eÂ±iw+A4eÂ±53iw),eÂ±2iw=âˆ’12eÂ±iw+32eÂ±53iwâˆ’w2(A5eÂ±53iw+512eÂ±2iw)âˆ“iw3(A6eÂ±53iwâˆ’118eÂ±2iw),Â±iweÂ±53iw=âˆ’1+eÂ±iwâˆ’w2(B1+10071620eÂ±iw)âˆ“iw3(B2+6791620eÂ±iw),Â±iweÂ±2iw=âˆ’32eÂ±iw+32eÂ±53iwâˆ’w2(49240eÂ±iw+B3eÂ±53iw)âˆ“iw3(29720eÂ±iw+B4eÂ±53iw),Â±iweÂ±2iw=âˆ’32eÂ±iw+32eÂ±53iwâˆ’w2(B3eÂ±53iw+4130eÂ±2iw)âˆ“iw3(B4eÂ±53iwâˆ’31180eÂ±2iw).

steps.instruction: The relation cos(w)=eiw+eâˆ’iw2 and sin(w)=eiwâˆ’eâˆ’iw2i are substituted in the equations Eq. (19) corresponding to y, we get trigonometric functions of w below:(20)cos(53w)=âˆ’23+53cos(w)âˆ’w2[A1+191486cos(w)]+118w3sin(w),sin(53w)=53sin(w)âˆ’191486w2sin(w)âˆ’w3[A2+118cos(w)],cos(2w)=âˆ’12cos(w)+32cos(53w)âˆ’w2[124cos(w)+A3cos(53w)]+w3[1144sin(w)+A4sin(53w)],sin(2w)=âˆ’12sin(w)+32sin(53w)âˆ’w2[124sin(w)+A3sin(53w)]âˆ’w3[1144cos(w)+A4cos(53w)],cos(2w)=âˆ’12cos(w)+32cos(53w)âˆ’w2[A5cos(53w)+512cos(2w)]+w3[A6sin(53w)âˆ’118sin(2w)],sin(2w)=âˆ’12sin(w)+32sin(53w)âˆ’w2[A5sin(53w)+512sin(2w)]âˆ’w3[A6cos(53w)âˆ’118cos(2w)],

steps.title: Trigonometrically-Fitted two-derivative linear multistep method
steps.instruction: In a similar manner, we incorporate the relationship between cos(w) and sin(w) into the equations Eq. (19), which correspond to hyâ€². As a result, we obtain trigonometric functions of w as follows:(22)wsin(53w)=1âˆ’cos(w)+w2[B1+10071620cos(w)]âˆ’6791620w3sin(w),wcos(53w)=sin(w)âˆ’10071620w2sin(w)âˆ’w3[B2+6791620cos(w)],wsin(2w)=32cos(w)âˆ’32cos(53w)+w2[49240cos(w)+B3cos(53w)]âˆ’w3[29720sin(w)+B4sin(53w)],wcos(2w)=âˆ’32sin(w)+32sin(53w)âˆ’w2[49240sin(w)+B3sin(53w)]âˆ’w3[29720cos(w)+B4cos(53w)],wsin(2w)=32cos(w)âˆ’32cos(53w)+w2[B5cos(53w)+4130cos(2w)]âˆ’w3[B6sin(53w)âˆ’31180sin(2w)],wcos(2w)=32sin(w)+32sin(53w)âˆ’w2[B5sin(53w)+4130sin(2w)]âˆ’w3[B6cos(53w)âˆ’31180cos(2w)].

steps.title: Trigonometrically-Fitted two-derivative linear multistep method
steps.instruction: As w approaches 0, the coefficientsAi and Bi,i=1,2,...,6 of the proposed methods using the fitting technique will return to their classical form values.

steps.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Here, we discuss the zero stability of block collocation method for solving second-order ODEs defined by [8] as follows:

steps.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Definition 1(Zero stable) A block multistep method with order p is zero stable provided the roots, Rl for l=0,1,...,m of the characteristic polynomial, Ïƒ(R) such that:(24)Ïƒ(R)=det[âˆ‘l=0mP(l)R(mâˆ’l)],P(0)=I, satisfy |Rl|â‰¤1 for l=0,1,...,m. If Rl is a repeated root, then the multiplicity of the root of modulus 1 must be at most 2, where I is identity matrix and P(l) is mÃ—m matrix.

steps.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: First, we use second-order test problem as follow:(25)yâ€³=âˆ’Î¸2y,Î¸>0.

steps.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Apply predictor formulae of TF-TDMC method into the test problem above and substitute w=Î¸h, we obtain(26)yn+53=(âˆ’23âˆ’A1w2)yn+(53âˆ’191486w2)yn+1+(âˆ’A2w2)hyâ€²n+(âˆ’118w2)hyâ€²n+1,(27)hyâ€²n+53=(âˆ’1âˆ’B1w2)yn+(1âˆ’10071620w2)yn+1+(âˆ’B2w2)hyâ€²n+(âˆ’6791620w2)hyâ€²n+1,(28)yn+2=(âˆ’12âˆ’124w2)yn+1+(32âˆ’A3w2)yn+53+(âˆ’1144w2)hyâ€²n+1+(âˆ’A4w2)hyâ€²n+53,(29)hyâ€²n+2=(âˆ’32âˆ’49240w2)yn+1+(32âˆ’B3w2)yn+53+(âˆ’1144w2)hyâ€²n+1+(âˆ’B4w2)hyâ€²n+53.

steps.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: We substitute (yn+2hyâ€²n+2)=R2,(yn+1hyâ€²n+1)=R and (ynhyâ€²n)=1, we yield the following first characteristic polynomial(32)Ïƒ(R,w)=(1001)R2âˆ’(M11M12M21M22)Râˆ’(N11N12N21N22).

steps.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Then we determine the determinant of the first characteristic polynomial and set w=0. This results in the following stability polynomial:(33)R4âˆ’2R3+R2=0.

steps.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Hence, the roots of stability polynomial are 0,0,1,1. All of the roots have modulus less or equal to one, which satisfied the zero stable conditions given in Definition 1. Thus, we conclude that the predictor formulae of TF-TDMC method is zero stable.

steps.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Next, we let Ïƒ(R,w)=0 and solve for R in term of w within the matrice, resulting in 2Ã—2 matrix P(w). The stability region in complex plane of TF-TDMC method can be defined as(34)RS={w:|Î»i(P(w))|<1,i=1,2},where Î»i are eigenvalues of P(w). The stability region of predictor for TF-TDMC method is shown in Fig. 1.Fig. 1The stability region of predictor for TF-TDMC method.Fig 1

steps.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Similarly, for analyzing corrector of TF-TDMC method, we can simply transform into the matrix form:(35)(1+512w2)yn+2=(âˆ’12)yn+1+(32âˆ’A5w2)yn+53+(âˆ’A6w2)hyâ€²n+53,(36)(1âˆ’31180w2)hyâ€²n+2=(âˆ’32)yn+1+(32âˆ’B5w2)yn+53+(âˆ’4130w2)yn+2+(âˆ’B6w2)hyâ€²n+53.

steps.title: Stability analysis of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Using the similar way, we determinant of first characteristic polynomial and set w=0. Stability polynomial is similar as Eq. (32), the roots of stability polynomial are 0,0,1,1. All the roots have modulus less or equal to one as well. It means that the corrector formulae of TF-TDMC method is zero stable. Both predictor and corrector method of TF-TDMC method are zero-stable. The stability region of corrector for TF-TDMC method is shown in Fig. 2.Fig. 2The stability region of corrector for TF-TDMC method.Fig 2

steps.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Step 1 Initialization

steps.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Start with an initial value for the dependent variable and its derivative, along with the initial conditions and step size, h for the problem. Identify the frequency, Î¸ of the problem and subsequently, determine the frequency-dependent parameters to be used in the predictor and corrector steps of the TF-TDMC method with the information of w=Î¸h.Step 2 Predictor Step

steps.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Use two-step predictor, based on previous values, to estimate the dependent variable, yn+k and its derivative, yâ€²n+k, k=53,2.Step 3 Corrector Step

steps.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Use the predicted values in a corrector formula to refine the estimates of. yn+2 and yâ€²n+2 with tolerance of 10âˆ’20. Check for convergence by comparing the predictor and corrector estimates, or by comparing the previous term with the current term in the corrector step. If they are sufficiently close, proceed; if not, iterate until convergence is achieved.Step 4 Iteration

steps.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Repeat the predictor and corrector steps for subsequent time steps, using the updated values from the corrector step as the starting point for the next iteration until reach to the endpoints.Step 5 Output

steps.title: Operation steps of TF-TDMC method
steps.instruction: Once the desired accuracy is achieved, output the values of the dependent variable at the specified time steps.
